body:
Add your name to stand with Democrats in this government shutdown
# [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117110383/575382332/938567526?refcode=header&noskip=true&&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDg1MjUwNy1mNWE2LWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=31DugVZnvM_FNMAaiyXQ6o4T_HEshYNJIMryFznWkAc=)
**🚨 BREAKING NEWS 🚨**
**[](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117110384/575382333/-309737922?refcode=251011_RG_shutdown-pet-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDg1MjUwNy1mNWE2LWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=31DugVZnvM_FNMAaiyXQ6o4T_HEshYNJIMryFznWkAc=)**
Hi, it’s Congressman Robert Garcia here with an urgent request.
The government is shut down, and the damage to our country is real — to federal workers, community services, and programs that so many families depend on.
But that’s not all. We’re now seeing Donald Trump use the federal government to weaponize that pain — blaming Democrats publicly through partisan messages and websites.
**That’s not just wrong — it’s likely illegal under the Hatch Act. And I’m calling for a full investigation into these violations.**
**[But I need to know where you stand, so let me know: Do you support Democrats investigating Trump’s lawless administration and working to hold him accountable for his abuses of power?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117110385/575382334/-1512507916?refcode=251011_RG_shutdown-pet-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDg1MjUwNy1mNWE2LWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=31DugVZnvM_FNMAaiyXQ6o4T_HEshYNJIMryFznWkAc=&emci=561f0d39-e4a6-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=50852507-f5a6-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=16877361)**
[ANSWER NOW](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117110386/575382335/-1512507916?refcode=251011_RG_shutdown-pet-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDg1MjUwNy1mNWE2LWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=31DugVZnvM_FNMAaiyXQ6o4T_HEshYNJIMryFznWkAc=&emci=561f0d39-e4a6-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=50852507-f5a6-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=16877361)
It’s simple: We must hold Trump accountable for his reckless attacks on the rule of law, democratic norms, and the constraints on his power.
If we cave now, we’d only be setting the stage for Trump to abuse his power without consequence even more in the future. We can’t let that happen.
**By standing together, we let Washington know that we won’t back down. That we won’t let fear and coercion win. And that we will defend our communities, our neighbors, and our future with all our strength.**
**[If you’re in, add your name to my petition and let me know you’re ready to stand up to Donald Trump at this critical moment for our democracy.>>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117110387/575382336/-1512507916?refcode=251011_RG_shutdown-pet-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDg1MjUwNy1mNWE2LWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=31DugVZnvM_FNMAaiyXQ6o4T_HEshYNJIMryFznWkAc=&emci=561f0d39-e4a6-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=50852507-f5a6-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=16877361)**
[ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/117110388/575382337/-1512507916?refcode=251011_RG_shutdown-pet-rs&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MTcxOSIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI1MDg1MjUwNy1mNWE2LWYwMTEtOGU2MS02MDQ1YmRlZDhiYTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=31DugVZnvM_FNMAaiyXQ6o4T_HEshYNJIMryFznWkAc=&emci=561f0d39-e4a6-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=50852507-f5a6-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=16877361)

Robert Garcia

subject: REPORT: DOJ sues Oregon
body:
https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251011em-bnor-rs [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251011em-bnor-rs]

FIGHT BACK >> [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251011em-bnor-rs]
The Trump Administration’s Department of Justice filed a lawsuit against the state of Oregon and Secretary of State Tobias Read.
Their demand? Access to Oregon’s voter registration list — including sensitive personal data like partial Social Security numbers and date of birth.
This is unprecedented. It’s targeted. And it’s dangerous.
Tobias has made it clear: He will not hand over Oregonians’ private data. He will stand up to the Trump administration’s DOJ, defend our elections, and protect your rights.
But let’s be clear: Tobias has gotten the attention of the Trump Administration for standing up to them. President Trump’s allies are trying to weaponize the federal government against him — and against every voter who values free and fair elections and privacy.
Grassroots support is Tobias’s firewall. That means us. That means you.
We need to demonstrate our support for Tobias in the face of this abuse of power.
Can you rush a donation right now to defend Oregon, protect our elections, and have Tobias’s back? [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251011em-bnor-rs]
DONATE NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/read-em-24?refcode=251011em-bnor-rs]
This is not normal. This is not safe. And we cannot let Trump and his cronies get away with it.

name: NDRC
subject: Trump’s DOJ asked the Supreme Court to weaken the Voting Rights Act
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the National Democratic Redistricting Committee, John Bisognano, President
body:
They’re coming for a crown jewel of the civil rights movement
| [](https://click.everyaction.com/k/117110693/575383696/-3547583?nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjEyZmI3NjAxLWY2YTYtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTYwNDViZGVkOGJhNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=AIQLn7TVrK7BDpxnyM4tgvn-g6ltguirs11JX8GEiz0=&emci=3387e63f-10a6-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&emdi=12fb7601-f6a6-f011-8e61-6045bded8ba4&ceid=8431045)
---

Peter, Donald Trump’s Justice Department is trying to weaken our voting process — again. Here’s what happened: The Trump administration filed an amicus brief in _Louisiana v. Callais_ — the case that calls into question the constitutionality of the Voting Rights Act (VRA) and Louisiana’s VRA-compliant map. In their argument, the Department of Justice urged the U.S. Supreme Court to end voting rights key protections — a move that infringes on the rights of voters of color and would make it easier for states to draw maps that gerrymander voters of color. This is yet another example of how the Trump administration is weaponizing the Department of Justice by coming for a crown jewel of the civil rights movement. Peter, they’re turning the DOJ’s long-standing track record of defending voting rights on its head. **That’s why the fair maps movement is committed to doing everything we can to hold the line against this assault on our democracy.[Will you rush a donation to make sure we can keep supporting fair representation across the country?](https://click.everyaction.com/k/117110694/575383697/-80723829?refcode=don_ndrc_tier1_email_TrumpAntiVotingRights_email_20251011-NDRC-Tier1-TrumpAntiVotingRights_&utm_source=email&utm_medium=email&utm_term=don&utm_content=20251011-NDRC-Tier1-TrumpAntiVotingRights_&utm_campaign=TrumpAntiVotingRights&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAxMy8xLzg5Mjk0IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjEyZmI3NjAxLWY2YTYtZjAxMS04ZTYxLTYwNDViZGVkOGJhNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=AIQLn7TVrK7BDpxnyM4tgvn-g6ltguirs11JX8GEiz0=)** | _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: They’re coming for a crown jewel of the civil rights movement | Peter, Donald Trump’s Justice Department is trying to weaken our voting process — again. Here’s what happened: The Trump administration filed an amicus brief in Louisiana v. Callais — the case that calls into question the constitutionality of the Voting Rights Act (VRA) and Louisiana’s VRA-compliant map. In their argument, the Department of Justice urged the U.S. Supreme Court to end voting rights key protections — a move that infringes on the rights of voters of color and would make it easier for states to draw maps that gerrymander voters of color. This is yet another example of how the Trump administration is weaponizing the Department of Justice by coming for a crown jewel of the civil rights movement. Peter, they’re turning the DOJ’s long-standing track record of defending voting rights on its head. That’s why the fair maps movement is committed to doing everything we can to hold the line against this assault on our democracy.= | If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: = | = = | = = | = Texas, Alabama, and beyond for fair maps and equal representation, and the fair maps movement has to be ready to respond. Former Chicago mayor and Obama's chief of staff, Rahm Emanuel penned an Op-Ed in the Wall Street Journal titled "Democrats Need an Education Reset" In that Op-Ed, Emanuel notes that for years that in polls, the party of education was always the Democrat party by more than 14 points and this tide has turned due to some things like the focus on gender and a conservative push to put children in schools following Covid and a demand for accountability, rather than a democrat blank check on education with no results. Rahm points out that:
"We've become so obsessed with bathroom access that we've ignored classroom excellence. America has lost the plot. Democrats need to refocus on the fundamentals in the elementary years--when it comes to high school, we need to be pursuing fundamental reform."
Emmanuel cites the Nation's Report Card on education results throughout the country and reading and math scores are below their 1992 numbers and says that this failing report card on education "should have lit Washington ablaze."
He goes on to state that "Few things are more important than ensuring every American student can read by third grade and do basic math by eighth" and the democrat method of throwing money at it is not working as Democrats have "written too many blank checks--particularly following Covid--without ensuring results or accountability for the investment".
Emmanuel blames the "the past five years debating pronouns without noticing that too many students can't tell you what a pronoun is" and that bathroom access has replaced "classroom excellence".
While Government cannot realize their failing report card (or take accountability for their failures), parents clearly see the immediate failures of education. By not returning to the classroom as soon as practicable after Covid was over, these delays, especially in Blue States was demonstrated by a lack of leadership. It has had everlasting developmental delays in our children.
Rahm goes on to say "Education reform is the seed corn of economic prosperity. America's growth following the Civil War was born in large part from Washington's investment in land-grant colleges. Early-20th-century growth flowed from the universal high-school movement. In the postwar era, the GI Bill was economic jet fuel. After Sputnik, new investments in science and technology were the key. But America's high-school model hasn't changed in 100 years, and other elements are proving hopelessly out of date. Facing the specter of China's growth and aggression, we need to mark the next historic reform era--today".
In this series of MALA - Making America Last Again, the Democrats are failing and this is pointed out in Education by Rahm, a Democrat who considers his "proudest achievement as Chicago's mayor was turning a public school system once labeled the nation's worst into one that Stanford researcher Sean Reardon described as "dramatically outperforming not just the other big poor districts, but almost every district in the country." We drove up graduation rates and test scores by focusing on the fundamentals. **The teachers union brass disagreed with my approach. That 's fine--the results speak for themselves."**
As we have previously written, by failing our children in education, the liberal democrats of today have screwed up education such that they are the real party of racism by keeping our children down. [See Article ](https://open.substack.com/pub/drmichaelgoldstein4ct/p/your-child-your-choice?r=26c3ku&utm_campaign=post&utm_medium=web&showWelcomeOnShare=false)
They are jeopardizing the future of our country by such a colossal systemic failure by keeping inner-city children down.
We are also seeing this in the race for Mayor of New York with Mamdani, the likely future mayor of NYC, who wants to perpetuate this education foley by eliminating gifted and talented programs. Those children that earn those seats at such schools (and are not given those seats) have a real chance for success.
The Democrats need a return to more leaders like Rahm Emanuel, a Democrat from a bygone era of what was a sensible party who is shining a light on the key educational failure of the modern democrat party and even challenged the unions when he implemented change in Chicago.
In Connecticut, the educational system is plagued by many of the issues that Rahm Emanuel raises, including the delayed opening of the schools during COVID at the insistence of the mighty all-powerful teacher's unions that are a major political machine for the democrats that run the Constitution State. **There is certainly more focus on pronouns and less on education here in Connecticut and in Mamdani 's Manhattan**.
Connecticut Democrats have been cutting the blank checks, the cost per student keeps rising, schools are built way over budget and we are not getting the results.
Bridgeport CT is one of the lowest ranked systems in the state. In a Democrat controlled city, rife with political corruption is failing the children and depriving them of the opportunities that a quality education would offer. Our economy is based on the concept that competition is the best option for quality and value. The Teacher's Union has had a noose over the Connecticut Democrats so that they perennially oppose school vouchers, which would give parents the choices and a future for their children.
Competition would also force the public schools to shape up and do a better job. The future of our country and our democracy depends on fixing our broken education system. The priority needs to be quality education that prepares our children to function in a 21stCentury economy and to understand why American Democracy is so special and worth preserving. We should take a few plays out of Rahm Emmanuel's playbook for our children to ensure a brighter future.

name: Team Guzman
subject: Moms Demand Action is with us— fighting for Virginia’s safety
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Elizabeth Guzman
body:
| | | Friend — Big news: Moms Demand Action Virginia has joined our campaign and endorsed me as the common-sense gun safety candidate in this race. This past weekend, we hit the doors together — talking to voters about what’s at stake and **why this election will determine the**** _future of gun laws in Virginia._** Here’s the truth:
* If we win in November, **we can finally sign gun safety legislation into law** , which will save countless lives across our Commonwealth.
* If we lose, MAGA extremists will make VA a gun hell scape — gutting background checks, undoing red flag laws, and putting more guns in our schools and streets.

My opponent has sided with the gun lobby every step of the way.**__****In fact, he has an ‘A’ rating from the NRA!** I’ve stood with Moms Demand Action since day one — **as a founding member of Moms Demand Action in Prince William County,** and as a delegate who believes our children’s right to safety comes before the gun lobby’s right to profit. But with less than a month to go, we need to make sure every voter knows the choice they have: **_common-sense safety or chaos._** ******[Can I count on you to help us finish strong and keep Virginians safe from gun violence?](https://click.actionnetwork.org/ss/c/u001.iIBo0N5fSFzEFL40Tn-vtbMETCUYClkOrOh9-SNJfqvhsqD6hnNNbCP7JMDskKO3DcwO3FPpifEsk6R5UXJAqeoZfkhx0rJYxvzeQyPAOXxPzaHpN7uxnA66Ao0W-7EqZyFw6iLW1nc6uNsMh9U_6L_ueOl5jQPiLidzriU9kwgt5cBdDXG2QBPgjRpUGHhTGsMarfYYB704UoMXia5mTW90ry6nMDIbF8B72GvGg8NSE-5cm5otXchpPbMge4CwkQKroq3Dzb8n_V1CwB3BXzKPSIZj9rAFxOcwPajVVF9svM2noilRbb0FPrASMjMTX6StVVqIteIxkFfAmdMlG2XNVeztQAD4VzVLKxEXA5RLkB3VYLQAHemeT4EzU-GmeelY9KpOhyX-EGsxkSGH6YXcTQAbVTsnSbYPMQcehuJ8al0sAK91kraj2IxU8aEi/4kn/HFr7LrVvQVW7euQJpy7pNw/h0/h001.ela0b8VhreE6J70sF0uMJ6H0RPN_c0ziScdG9kzp5Gc)** _If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately._ [Donate $10

clean_body: | | | Friend — Big news: Moms Demand Action Virginia has joined our campaign and endorsed me as the common-sense gun safety candidate in this race. This past weekend, we hit the doors together — talking to voters about what’s at stake and why this election will determine the future of gun laws in Virginia. Here’s the truth: If we win in November, we can finally sign gun safety legislation into law , which will save countless lives across our Commonwealth. If we lose, MAGA extremists will make VA a gun hell scape — gutting background checks, undoing red flag laws, and putting more guns in our schools and streets. My opponent has sided with the gun lobby every step of the way.In fact, he has an ‘A’ rating from the NRA! I’ve stood with Moms Demand Action since day one — as a founding member of Moms Demand Action in Prince William County, and as a delegate who believes our children’s right to safety comes before the gun lobby’s right to profit. But with less than a month to go, we need to make sure every voter knows the choice they have: common-sense safety or chaos. **** If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately. Thank you to every volunteer, advocate, and mom fighting alongside me for safer communities. Let’s show the nation that Virginia refuses to go backward. In solidarity, Elizabeth | Democrat is running for Delegate in Virginia's 22nd District. She came to America as a single mother, becoming a union leader and one of the first Latinas in the Virginia General Assembly. She passed stronger gun safety laws, expanded collective bargaining rights and paid sick leave, and protected reproductive rights. # [Who made the Gaza peace plan possible?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=175913173&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc1OTEzMTczLCJpYXQiOjE3NjAyMjY4MjAsImV4cCI6MTc2MjgxODgyMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Cknv4Jep-oHesK9YVZPH6ZdC9fRsMTVUYphjNYgoH0U)
### An international debate over the Nobel Peace Prize overlooked voices standing in solidarity and bearing witness to international crimes
| | [Shahid Buttar](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---
| Oct 11
---
| [](https://substack.com/@shahidbuttar)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=175913173&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc1OTEzMTczLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc2MDIyNjgyMCwiZXhwIjoxNzYyODE4ODIwLCJpc3MiOiJwdWItMTAxODU5NyIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.sQH_7FLDBi-SU7Z6ULVtPr2IUZm72LGBqXT9Bm1Tfbo&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=175913173&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc1OTEzMTczLCJpYXQiOjE3NjAyMjY4MjAsImV4cCI6MTc2MjgxODgyMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Cknv4Jep-oHesK9YVZPH6ZdC9fRsMTVUYphjNYgoH0U&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=175913173&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc1OTEzMTczLCJpYXQiOjE3NjAyMjY4MjAsImV4cCI6MTc2MjgxODgyMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Cknv4Jep-oHesK9YVZPH6ZdC9fRsMTVUYphjNYgoH0U)
---
| | [](https://substack..LiqeAEIHnU_d7No8aFw1_K0_lFtxBUbS2ouZhYKVR3Y?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/shahidbuttar/p/who-made-the-gaza-peace-plan-possible?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

Before the recent announcement of the "[Gaza peace plan](https://substack.com/redirect/c880ff42-b456-4434-a106-1e2dd45437a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)," I wrote a few pieces about the flotilla missions aiming to deliver desperately needed humanitarian aid to Gaza despite the criminal Israeli genocide. Among other things, those pieces explored the [possibility](https://substack.com/redirect/d5980394-75df-455c-8b1d-4d2b7b73d5b8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) of international solidarity with Gaza [expanding](https://substack.com/redirect/8aeb3bb7-2633-4180-9926-c67ea32b2773?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in the wake of a U.S. labor leader [enduring](https://substack.com/redirect/324c966f-446e-4afe-b496-3baf2f440ef8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) discriminatory state violence.
The [latest unarmed flotilla mission intercepted](https://substack.com/redirect/5095c70f-6532-47fb-b12f-fe31dcd575e6?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by the Israeli Navy may have played a bigger role in compelling the peace plan than most observers realize. Coming on the heels of previous efforts, it confronted Israeli belligerence, which prompted a crucial international reaction whose impacts remain largely unobserved.
_Help inform --and inspire--your friends by sharing this post._
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=175913173&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc1OTEzMTczLCJpYXQiOjE3NjAyMjY4MjAsImV4cCI6MTc2MjgxODgyMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Cknv4Jep-oHesK9YVZPH6ZdC9fRsMTVUYphjNYgoH0U)
While many debated whether or not [another U.S. president](https://substack.com/redirect/aff55c44-e766-4dc1-ada2-5eb6c2a3fe85?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) deserved to win the Nobel Peace Prize, few recognized the events that likely played a bigger role in forcing Israel to accept the ceasefire long (ineffectually) demanded by the international community.
### **What solidarity looks like**
Solidarity references action rooted in the recognition of shared interests. It evokes the power of collective action, as well as the profound service implicit in standing alongside others to challenge abuses to which we ourselves have not (yet) been subjected.
It might come as no surprise, then, to see a [visionary Black U.S. labor leader](https://substack.com/redirect/324c966f-446e-4afe-b496-3baf2f440ef8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) joining the Sumud flotilla (named after an Arabic word connoting [steadfast perseverance](https://substack.com/redirect/890ba82b-81d9-4428-87e1-4bb31814616d?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)) and serving as one of the flotilla's spokespeople. The detention of Chris Smalls by Israeli authorities, who then [subjected him to state violence](https://substack.com/redirect/e4c4d3a3-4170-468f-8923-d21bce952345?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in custody, is not far removed from the vicious attacks on civil rights organizers by authorities across the South--and in Washington, DC--during the Jim Crow era.
It has more relevance to today's context than most observers realized before Trump's [return](https://substack.com/redirect/79f7c372-fdfd-428c-bdb0-1508d9697c94?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to Washington earlier this year.
The flotilla [intercepted](https://substack.com/redirect/1b5dc532-ce2a-4faa-b05a-f4dc1e3b5db3?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) last week mobilized a massive--and widely representative--[global base of support](https://substack.com/redirect/e2be066a-9d58-46ec-a9f5-b9db99164309?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). It consisted of over 40 ships, and 500 participants coming from over 44 different countries. Noteworthy participants included an elected policymaker from Portugal, the grandson of Nelson Mandela, the former Mayor of Barcelona, and climate justice spokesperson and international (and intergenerational) hero Greta Thunberg.
| [](https://substack.com/redirect/f25586be-9ed6-4b56-96bd-2c4920f8c5c2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)|
---|---|---
Greta Thunberg upon landing in Greece after enduring abuse in Israeli custody
### **What heroism looks like**
Thunberg's participation in the flotilla is noteworthy for several reasons.
_Learning anything? Don 't stop now! Subscriptions are free._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..-Y0FXRad7aaTh4FRxaMq9d6H2dl-XS_gUsEXlTeIqqM?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=175913173)
First, Israel would likely have embraced even [more severe violence](https://substack.com/@caitlinjohnstone/note/c-162133993?r=97w99&utm_medium=ios&utm_source=notes-share-action) in its response to the flotilla had white Europeans not been on board. According to the head of Amnesty International, the interception was a "[calculated act of intimidation](https://substack.com/redirect/3102bcb6-64f2-4c95-ac75-fcba648f18f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) . . . intended to punish and silence critics of Israel's genocide and its unlawful blockade on Gaza." Without the presence of White Europeans, that intimidation would likely have been as lethal as [other Israeli operations](https://substack.com/redirect/3ff2586b-fa7c-44d4-bea1-e2acda134f07?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) falsely described as defensive.
To be fair, even [white Americans have been killed](https://substack.com/redirect/00d96d36-0619-4155-bef4-09b397a3a48b?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) by Israeli soldiers with impunity. On the one hand, that might suggest limits to the value of international solidarity. That view, however, mistakes the ways in which movements build over time, and how [voices speaking in one era can inspire](https://substack.com/redirect/fc7e5fbf-8c93-4c98-992c-09027bedb608?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) those who come after.
In retrospect, the prescience and courage of Rachel Corrie may have inspired the more recent self-immolation of [U.S. servicemember Aaron Bushnell](https://substack.com/redirect/4ac26d33-0d89-4d14-8b35-a33900ca1c86?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg). Few have appreciated the unique challenges confronting military servicemembers, whose choices illustrate the moral conundrum that so many of us confront in different ways.
Servicemembers, in particular, are trapped between a proverbial rock and a hard place. By enlisting, they sacrifice their First Amendment rights, which effectively blocks them from publicly speaking out to challenge the crimes of their leaders. Meanwhile, following orders supporting an ongoing genocide risks the specter of becoming complicit in international crimes.
If servicemembers disobey orders in order to comply with international legal principles settled at Nuremberg, they risk a court-martial (soon). On the other hand, if they follow those illegal orders, they risk international liability for human rights violations (later).
That is a faustian choice, which makes the acts of solidarity by civilians so crucial. Few demonstrate that example better than [Chris Smalls](https://substack.com/redirect/324c966f-446e-4afe-b496-3baf2f440ef8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and Greta Thunberg.
### **Intersectionality**
Second, Thunberg, in particular, demonstrates the intersectionality of the various issues visible in today's accelerating crises.
Some might imagine climate justice to be separable from human rights struggles. They overlook the extent to which climate chaos has been largely enabled by strategic denials of human rights. The recurring tragic experiences of [populations](https://substack.com/redirect/20c4eb34-3935-4633-aa9e-0b8d945e2ddd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) living in [regions](https://substack.com/redirect/43fac121-d9cd-4e46-87aa-b7b3ba9164c0?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) rich in fossil fuel resources offer compelling case studies.
The West was warned of this dynamic decades ago. The late Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. explained that capitalism (i.e., industrial resource extraction) inevitably intersects (i.e., relies upon and also reinforces) militarism and racism. His [recognition of intersectionality](https://substack.com/redirect/48433a9d-3225-4317-9846-35b7be68b1de?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) is among the most [profound gifts](https://substack.com/redirect/87a5fe8d-2acc-4518-b304-400e86e102fd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that he left us.
Few stand in the footsteps of Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. with as much consistency as Thunberg. In that respect, her example might be poised to help others finally recognize [his radical legacy](https://substack.com/redirect/87a5fe8d-2acc-4518-b304-400e86e102fd?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)--and ideally, [take action](https://substack.com/redirect/7d3ad1bd-7ddc-454d-a28c-8eb196b5be0a?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) to support its realization in practice.
[Share](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=1018597&post_id=175913173&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTc1OTEzMTczLCJpYXQiOjE3NjAyMjY4MjAsImV4cCI6MTc2MjgxODgyMCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTEwMTg1OTciLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.Cknv4Jep-oHesK9YVZPH6ZdC9fRsMTVUYphjNYgoH0U)
If nothing else, Greta has also experienced a pattern similar to what MLK encountered during his era. She was widely welcomed when she challenged climate nihilism--but equally widely condemned when she stepped forward to challenge the Israeli genocide. Similarly, MLK was lauded as a civil rights hero by many voices that turned their backs on him once he began to assertively challenge Washington's war on Vietnam and the militarism that enabled it.
### **Intergenerational leadership**
Third, Thunberg embodies the generational change impeded in the U.S. by decades of [continuing bipartisan corruption](https://substack.com/redirect/b0777b23-7e2b-4422-9bb9-1a2bc00ba3e7?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) in Washington. She emerged as a global grassroots leader as a teenager, and remains in her early 20s. Her voice resonates with young people in a way that the geriatric organizers of climate chaos cannot.
Before she was detained, she recorded [a short video](https://substack.com/redirect/801c8127-738d-4a4b-813f-56fe828496eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that I encourage every reader to [watch](https://substack.com/redirect/801c8127-738d-4a4b-813f-56fe828496eb?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
The aid that the flotilla aimed to deliver was crucially important because Israel had imposed a [deliberate starvation campaign](https://substack.com/redirect/56ecaa9c-0a99-4271-a553-58633c3659ff?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) as part of its genocide. While many [concerns have emerged](https://substack.com/redirect/c880ff42-b456-4434-a106-1e2dd45437a8?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) about the legitimacy and durability of the nascent peace plan, the most immediate way in which it has changed life on the ground for Gazans is by re-opening the [delivery of humanitarian aid](https://substack.com/redirect/4319e3e5-88ca-491b-879e-16ceccd37429?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg).
Amnesty International's Secretary General Agnes Callamard [explained](https://substack.com/redirect/3102bcb6-64f2-4c95-ac75-fcba648f18f9?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) that Israel's interception of the Sumud flotilla:
> comes after weeks of threats and incitement by Israeli officials against the flotilla and its participants and after several attempts to sabotage some of its ships....The very fact that they had to set sail in the first place is a clear indictment of the international community's persistent failure to end Israel's ongoing genocide and to ensure the unhindered flow of aid to Palestinians in the Gaza Strip.
In this context, the flotilla may have focused international attention on an unfolding genocide, in a way that years of Israeli bombing campaigns--and sniper attacks on unarmed children--somehow did not.
Many voices have debated in the past few days whether President Trump deserved the Nobel Peace Prize based on his diplomacy between Israel and Gaza. They all overlooked the far more influential impact of international solidarity--and the millions of people of conscience from around the world who have taken action to challenge the Israeli genocide and defend human rights in exile.
### **Swift global reaction inspired a growing wave**
Around the world, reaction to Israel's assault on the flotilla was both swift and dramatic.
In both [Italy](https://substack.com/@emangaza/note/c-162033127?r=97w99&utm_medium=ios&utm_source=notes-share-action) and [Germany](https://substack.com/@deaglanomulrooney/note/c-162111603?r=97w99&utm_medium=ios&utm_source=notes-share-action), activists shut down mass transit networks, while calls mounted across Europe for an ensuing general strike. The government of Colombia [expelled Israeli diplomats](https://substack.com/redirect/64aa483b-6e58-40ad-b16c-ffb5a7dfeac5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) and cancelled its free trade agreement with Israel, while Turkish officials described the Israeli assault on the flotilla as "an act of terrorism." A [litany of other countries](https://substack.com/redirect/64aa483b-6e58-40ad-b16c-ffb5a7dfeac5?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) witnessed either grassroots action, condemnation by officials of Israeli actions, or both.
Trump's assaults on the rights of Americans--particularly visible at the moment in Chicago, where I lived throughout the 1990s--is largely intended to diminish our collective capacity to model these examples from Europe and elsewhere. Whether it proved successful would have been as much up to us as it would have been to the authorities who aim to compel our complacence.
In that context, I see the announcement of a "Gaza peace plan" as akin to George W. Bush's speech on May 1, 2003 before a "[mission accomplished](https://substack.com/redirect/8000ba9e-a10a-4e90-aef9-5fdd45524d1f?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg)" banner hung from the U.S.S. Abraham Lincoln. Both were measures taken theoretically to advance human rights, but particularly, to hamstring domestic dissent and derail mounting international condemnation.
### **A preview of some upcoming posts**
I started a [series](https://substack.com/redirect/e1e57d5d-dac8-4e49-9363-8cb3168b4128?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) earlier this year [examining](https://substack.com/redirect/b7c0aa04-7261-4e0a-82c9-70221af445b2?j=eyJ1IjoiMWh2cjYwIn0.VBabVq-VHATXCJqsddI7uMpdiTm0WkVanRDZfvCWMkg) the impact of Trump's policies on the U.S. economy. Finishing it has stymied me, but I've got a draft in motion of the next installment that I hope to finish developing soon.
I've also been working on pieces exploring the effective imposition of martial law in Chicago, and a further analysis of this week's international discussion surrounding the Nobel Peace Prize. While I'm the first to argue that Trump's domestic violations of civil rights and civil liberties should disqualify him for any discussion of the award, I'll make a case that he might have ironically deserved it--for reasons very different than he and his confused supporters seem to think.
_Want to support my work? The next paid subscriber to Chronicles of a Dying Empire will be the 70th supporter enabling writing that 21,000 other readers enjoy --and learn from--for free._

Finally, I've got a piece about a bizarre alignment that emerged between the far left and far right unnoticed by the centrist voices whose gaslighting brought them together. I might ultimately make it a bonus section for paid subscribers rather than a standalone article, but we'll see how many topics emerge to further distract me between now and whenever it comes to rest.

clean_body: This campaign is about Alaskans, NOT headlines.| | | Click here if you wish to unsubscribe Hey, Peter! Did you catch Nancy's announcement this week? She officially launched her campaign to be the next governor of Alaska, and the outpouring of support has been amazing. Check out our to hear from Nancy directly about this race. And if you can, chip in $20 or whatever you can afford to help Nancy win! [ [ WATCH & JOIN THE TEAM ] ]() Thanks for helping us kick off this campaign right. Let's win this campaign the Alaska way, with hard work, grit, and honesty. Michael Digital Director, Nancy Dahlstrom for Governor | | | | | ---|---|---
